title: Pastinaca schelkovnikowii
taxonomy: Pastinaca schelkovnikowii is a species of flowering plant in the carrot family Apiaceae. It is native to Central Asia. The plant is classified as a member of the genus Pastinaca, which includes several other species of wild parsnip. Pastinaca schelkovnikowii is further classified as a member of the subfamily Apioideae and the family Apiaceae. The exact classification may vary depending on the source or latest botanical research.

The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat various ailments such as fever, colds, and respiratory issues. Its root and leaves are used to make teas and infusions that are believed to have antiseptic and antibacterial effects.
